page_content:
ENCE718 Advanced Structural Systems (3 Credits) Review of classical determinate and indeterminate analysis technique; multistory buildings; space structures; suspension bridges and cables structures; arches; long span bridges. Formerly: ENCE750.

ENCE721 Investment Theory for Project Engineers (3 Credits) An introductory course covering investment theory and its application to project evaluation and selection. Selected topics include: basic theory of interest and ﬁxed income securities; portfolio selection and modiﬁcation; capital asset pricing; asset price dynamics; derivative securities; and project evaluation using real options. Credit Only Granted for: ENCE652 or ENCE721. Formerly: ENCE652.

ENCE722 Market, Spatial, and Trafﬁc Equilibrium Models in Project Management (3 Credits) Introduction to equilibrium models involving economics and engineering. Topics include: review of relevent optimization theory; the nonlinear complementary problem (NCP) and variational inequality problem formats to solve equilibrium problems; review of relevant game theory, equilibrium models, and algorithims. Credit Only Granted for: ENCE654, ENCE688M, or ENCE722. Formerly: ENCE654.

ENCE688 Advanced Topics in Civil Engineering (1-3 Credits) Advanced topics selected by the faculty from the current literature of civil engineering to suit the needs and background of students. May be taken for repeated credit when identiﬁed by topic title.
